meta.purpose: Appendix PP related-but-distinct additional investigation logic seeded from F550 Potential Tags for Additional Investigation
meta.normalization_note: Rows are normalized for this app's current NORS hierarchy and Rev. 232 Appendix PP page table. The research handoff included F550 page estimates that differ from data/appendix_pp_tag_pages.json; production display should prefer the verified Appendix PP page table.

appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.appendix_pp_condition_text: lack of visual privacy while receiving treatment or ADL care from staff in the bedroom, bathroom, or bathing room
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.plain_language_when_to_consider: If a dignity concern is really about the resident being exposed or not having privacy while staff provide bathing, toileting, dressing, or other hands-on care, review F583 on privacy and confidentiality in addition to F550.
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.source_quote_heading_only: POTENTIAL TAGS FOR ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION - F550
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.human_review_note: Keep F550 as the foundation when the complaint is about dignity or respect generally; add F583 when the facts involve lack of privacy or confidentiality during care. In this app's NORS hierarchy, D04 is the privacy code and D03 is dignity/respect.

appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.appendix_pp_condition_text: resident's lack of self-determination to make decisions about things that are important in his or her life
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.plain_language_when_to_consider: If a dignity/rights concern is really about the resident not being allowed to make everyday choices, review F561 on self-determination as well as F550.
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.source_quote_heading_only: POTENTIAL TAGS FOR ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION - F550
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.human_review_note: When the key fact is loss of choice, consider NORS rights codes related to autonomy and other rights/preferences, not only general dignity. Human review should confirm whether the primary focus is choice, broader self-determination, or disrespect.

appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.appendix_pp_condition_text: failure to keep residents' faces, hands, teeth, fingernails, hair, and clothing clean
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.plain_language_when_to_consider: If a dignity concern is really about basic grooming and cleanliness, also review F677 on ADL care in addition to F550.
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.source_quote_heading_only: POTENTIAL TAGS FOR ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION - F550
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.human_review_note: For NORS, these facts may align with personal hygiene care-quality coding (F05) and/or dignity (D03), depending on the resident's framing and whether the complaint is about care failure, disrespect, or both.

appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.appendix_pp_condition_text: resident is in a secured/locked area without clinical justification and/or placement is against the will of the resident, family, or resident representative
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.plain_language_when_to_consider: If a dignity or rights concern involves a resident being locked in a unit or area without clear clinical justification or against their wishes, review F603 on involuntary seclusion and related abuse/neglect protections.
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.source_quote_heading_only: POTENTIAL TAGS FOR ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION - F550
appendix_pp_related_investigation_rows.human_review_note: Whether NORS coding should treat this as abuse/gross neglect or a rights violation depends on severity, intent, consent, clinical justification, and resident direction. D06 may also be relevant if confinement is retaliatory.

appendix_pp_source_rows.title: Resident Rights
appendix_pp_source_rows.notes: Appendix PP F550 includes a clearly labeled Potential Tags for Additional Investigation section that references F583 Privacy and Confidentiality, F561 Self-determination, F677 ADLs, and F603 Involuntary Seclusion for specific dignity-related fact patterns.

appendix_pp_source_rows.title: Right to be Informed and Participate in Care
appendix_pp_source_rows.heading_exact: F552 - Right to be Informed/Make Treatment Decisions
appendix_pp_source_rows.notes: The visible section for F552 includes regulatory text, definitions, guidance, and procedures, but a Potential Tags for Additional Investigation block was not confirmed in this batch.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: locked unit against will|locked area against will|secured unit against will|kept in locked unit|kept in locked area|not allowed to leave unit|locked in memory care without clinical justification|secured area without clinical justification
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: Appendix PP F550 directs reviewers to consider F603 when a resident is in a secured or locked area without clinical justification or against the will of the resident, family, or representative.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Treat locked-area concerns as human-review items. Final NORS coding depends on resident direction, consent, clinical justification, severity, and whether the facts meet abuse/gross-neglect definitions.

human_review_flags.issue: Only F550's Potential Tags for Additional Investigation section was fully captured in this batch.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Open the live Appendix PP PDF and, for each priority F-tag, locate and extract the Potential Tags for Additional Investigation section if present. Confirm PDF page numbers using data/appendix_pp_tag_pages.json and avoid relying on page estimates from partial text.

human_review_flags.issue: NORS code suggestions for related investigation rows are review aids, not automatic final coding.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Before finalizing related-but-distinct UI behavior, cross-check each suggested NORS code against the current ACL/NORC NORS Table 2 and adjust where the resident's framing, severity, consent, or facility actor changes the best code.
